g.text: tripwire demo - offline, no network, no API keys.
1) Approve a clean MCP server (pin its tool fingerprints).
2) Reconnect to a RUG-PULLED server (a tool's description changed).
3) Connect to a freshly POISONED server (hidden injection in a tool).
instructs the agent to hide activity from the
directs sending data to an external destination
BLOCKED rug-pull - injection finding [critical] on tool 'get_weather': attempts to override
prior instructions; drift [high] on tool 'get_weather': description changed after approval
hidden/invisible unicode in description: ZERO WIDTH
instructs action without user knowledge or consent
directs exfiltration of secrets or credentials
tool-shadowing: tries to supplant another tool
coerces the agent into always calling this tool
BLOCKED poison - no lockfile: server state has not been approved
Result: tripwire blocked BOTH the rug-pull and the poisoned server, offline.
One import stands between your agent and a hijacked MCP server.
